chief_complaint: A 61-year-old male presented with anterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) and chest pain, diaphoresis, and dyspnea.
history: The patient underwent successful primary percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) to the left anterior descending artery. He was readmitted with chest pain, diaphoresis, and dyspnea. Initial echocardiography demonstrated preserved apical function.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia, tachypnea. Physical examination: chest pain, diaphoresis, and dyspnea.
labs: ECG: anterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI). Echocardiography: severe left ventricular dysfunction with apical ballooning. Coronary angiography: patent stents.
